# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# The id of the broker. This must be set to a unique integer for each broker.
broker.id=0

############################# Socket Server Settings #############################
# The address the socket server listens on. It will get the value returned by
# java.net.InetAddress.getCanonicalHostName() if not configured.
#   FORMAT:
#     listeners = listener_name://host_name:port
#   EXAMPLE:
#     listeners = PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092
listeners=PLAINTEXT://:9092,SSL://:9093

# Hostname and port the broker will advertise to producers and consumers. If not set,
# it uses the value for "listeners".
#advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your.advertised.host.name:9092

# Listeners to publish in ZooKeeper for clients to use. If not set, all listeners will be published.
#listener.security.protocol.map=PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT,SSL:SSL

# The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network and sending responses to the network
num.network.threads=3

# The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests, which may include disk I/O
num.io.threads=8

# The send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) used by the socket server
socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400

# The receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) used by the socket server
socket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400

# The maximum size of a request that the server will allow (this specifies the maximum size of the request header and the request body).
socket.request.max.bytes=104857600


############################# Log Basics #############################
# A comma separated list of directories under which to store log files
log.dirs=/kafka/logs/data1,/kafka/logs/data2

# The default number of log partitions per topic. More partitions allow greater parallelism for consumption by increasing the number of consumers that can concurrently consume from a topic.
num.partitions=1

# The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown.
num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=1

############################# Log Flush Policy #############################
# The number of messages after which to force a log flush
#log.flush.interval.messages=10000

# The maximum time in ms that a message can remain un-flushed in a log before being flushed to disk
#log.flush.interval.ms=1000

############################# Log Retention Policy #############################
# The minimum age of a log file to be eligible for deletion due to age
log.retention.hours=168

# A size-based retention policy for logs. Segments are deleted from the log when the sum of their sizes exceeds this value.
#log.retention.bytes=1073741824

# The maximum size of a log segment file. When this size is reached a new log segment will be created.
log.segment.bytes=1073741824

# The interval at which log segments are checked to see if they can be deleted according to the retention policies
log.retention.check.interval.ms=300000


############################# Zookeeper #############################
# Zookeeper connection string (see zookeeper docs for details).
# This is a comma separated host:port pairs, each corresponding to a zk
# server. e.g. "127.0.0.1:3000,127.0.0.1:3001,127.0.0.1:3002".
# You can also append an optional chroot string to the zookeeper connection
# string to specify the root directory for all kafka data.
zookeeper.connect=zk1.example.com:2181,zk2.example.com:2181,zk3.example.com:2181

# Timeout in ms for connecting to zookeeper
zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=6000


############################# Group Coordinator Settings #############################
# The maximum time that the group coordinator will wait for a consumer to send a heartbeat
# before marking the consumer as dead and rebalancing the group.
group.max.session.timeout.ms=60000

# The minimum time that the group coordinator will wait for a consumer to send a heartbeat
# before marking the consumer as dead and rebalancing the group.
group.min.session.timeout.ms=6000

############################# Replication Settings #############################
# The number of replicas each partition should have. If not set, the default is 1.
default.replication.factor=3

# The minimum number of replicas that must acknowledge a write for the write to be considered successful.
min.insync.replicas=2

# The time a replica can be behind the leader before it is considered out of sync.
replica.lag.time.max.ms=30000

# The frequency with which the log cleaner checks if any log segments can be cleaned.
log.cleaner.enable=true
log.cleaner.threads=2
log.cleaner.io.max.bytes.per.second=104857600
log.cleaner.dedupe.buffer.size=134217728

# The maximum time a log segment will be held in the log cleaner's dedupe buffer before being flushed.
log.cleaner.backoff.ms=15000

# The ratio of log cleaner's log retention to the log's total size.
log.cleaner.min.cleanable.dirty.ratio=0.5

############################# Auto Create Topics #############################
# Enable auto creation of topics.
auto.create.topics.enable=false

############################# SSL Configuration #############################
# Uncomment and configure these for SSL listener
#ssl.keystore.location=/path/to/kafka.keystore.jks
#ssl.keystore.password=your_keystore_password
#ssl.key.password=your_key_password
#ssl.truststore.location=/path/to/kafka.truststore.jks
#ssl.truststore.password=your_truststore_password
#ssl.client.auth=required # or 'requested' or 'none'

############################# Metrics #############################
# The JMX port to use for publishing broker metrics.
#jmx.port=9999
#jmx.hostname=your.broker.hostname
